text: Robert — morning walk ran long. Judith spotted a roadrunner on the greenbelt and we lost ten minutes. Still making 9 AM, but just.
Ha, Judith and her wildlife detours. See you at the desk.
She's documented every bird in the Scottsdale preserve. I'm just the escort.
Does she know the aviation gallery better than you yet?
She walked through it once and explained wake turbulence to a kid better than I did. I've stopped being surprised.
I can cover the Thursday morning shift this week. Walt and I moved our tee time to the afternoon, so I'm free until noon.
Thank you, Paul. I'll put you down for Thursday 9 to noon and move Howard to next week.
For the 737 cockpit station, I've been telling visitors the flaps and slats story when they ask why the wing changes shape on landing. Lands well with the kids.
That's a good one. Mind writing it up for the talking-points sheet? Newer docents would use it.
Will do. I'll have it to you before Thursday.
Flight-sim kiosk touchscreen is dead in the lower-right corner again. Visitors can't finish the takeoff sequence. Front desk has a sign on it for now.
Got it. I'll reseat the digitizer cable this afternoon and open a ticket to track it.
Happy to run a refresher for docents on the 737 panel scenarios. I flew the line on these for years, so I can keep it practical.
That would be great. Let's pick a Tuesday after the morning rush.
Shot 82 at Grayhawk this morning. Walt insists the wind was a factor. It was not the wind.
There's always a factor. Coffee's on me Thursday.
I can give the short talk on the museum's 737 history before the evening lecture if you still need a speaker. Fifteen minutes, no slides.
Perfect, you're on the program. I'll add you right before Margaret's archive segment.
Welcome aboard to our new Tuesday docents. If anyone wants to shadow the aviation gallery first, find me at the desk and I'll walk you through it.
My audio-guide tablet won't join the staff Wi-Fi this morning. Same one that gave Rebecca trouble last week.
Covering the Tuesday simulator exhibit this week - Carol's out, I've got the 9 AM school group.
Docent meeting moved to Thursday after shift. Agenda is the B-17 tour rotation.
Kudos to Linda for reworking the school-tour safety page. Much clearer now.
Reminder: simulator software update ran overnight, please log any glitches in this channel.
Front desk is short Saturday for the gala setup - can anyone flex an hour?
The Tuskegee Airmen panels go up Monday. Floor will be busy Tuesday, plan tours accordingly.
Handbook edits for the wide-body section are in the drive, review before the board meeting.
Restoration crew needs a volunteer photographer for the engine cowling progress shots.
School group of 40 fifth-graders Thursday. Splitting into two tours, I'll take the sim half.
Gift shop restocked the model 777 kits the kids love. Heads up for the tour endings.
Anyone free to help catalog the new donation of flight logs in the research library?
Great turnout for the aviation-history talk. Coffee's on in the break room, come find it.
